headline: Asian stocks drop again as rollercoaster week draws to close
summary: Asian tech stocks sank again Friday as the rollercoaster ride that has characterised the week continued into the weekend, while crude prices edged back down after a brief rally sparked by news of an attack on a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.Oil prices resumed the downward shift that has marked the week, having risen around two percent Thursday on news that a cargo ship was damaged by an unknown projectile off Oman's coast in Hormuz.

headline: Apple hikes prices as memory chip costs skyrocket
summary:
STORY: :: Apple

Apple is hiking prices as the cost of memory chips skyrockets.

Its lowest priced laptop will jump from $599 to $699, with iPad prices also rising.

For now at least, the iPhone is unaffected.

Apple says it can no longer avoid passing on rising costs to consumers.

The price of memory chips jumped by around 98% in the first quarter, and has continued to soar ever since.

That is driven by the boom in AI data centers.

Companies like Nvidia have signed big deals with chipmakers, leaving consumer electronics firms scrambling to secure supplies.

Apple said it had never seen component prices increase so much, so quickly.

While iPhones weren’t hit by Thursday’s move, analysts say it’s only a matter of time before they too face price increases.

Apple shares fell almost 5%, while Dell dropped 8%.

Some industry watchers say Apple’s rivals may have to do even bigger price increases, as they lack the Mac maker’s deep ties to suppliers.

Research firm IDC says the global smartphone and computer markets will both see sharp contractions this year due to the rising prices.

Research firm IDC says the global smartphone and computer markets will both see sharp contractions this year due to the rising prices.

headline: Exclusive-Apple supplier Tata tightens internal controls after data breach, sources say
summary: Tata Electronics, a key Indian supplier to Apple, has restricted internal access to sensitive systems as it investigates a leak of thousands of secret client files on the dark web, a Tata source and two industry officials said.  Tata has ‌also hired a global consultant to conduct a forensic audit and has reported the incident to the Indian government and its clients, said the Tata ‌source, declining to be named given the sensitivity of the matter.  Reuters reported this week that ransomware group World Leaks posted more than 200,000 files to the dark web, including purported component design papers from Apple and ​Tesla, both of which are Tata clients.

content.title: Exclusive-Apple supplier Tata tightens internal controls after data breach, sources say
content.summary: Tata Electronics, a key Indian supplier to Apple, has restricted internal access to sensitive systems as it investigates a leak of thousands of secret client files on the dark web, a Tata source and two industry officials said.  Tata has ‌also hired a global consultant to conduct a forensic audit and has reported the incident to the Indian government and its clients, said the Tata ‌source, declining to be named given the sensitivity of the matter.  Reuters reported this week that ransomware group World Leaks posted more than 200,000 files to the dark web, including purported component design papers from Apple and ​Tesla, both of which are Tata clients.

headline: The Week in Numbers: Micron climbs high, Apple raises prices
summary:
STORY: From Micron's mega market cap to Apple's price hikes...

...this is the Week in Numbers.

:: The Week in Numbers

:: $1.4 trillion.

About $1.4 trillion was Micron's market cap after it briefly blew past the value of Meta and Tesla for the first time.

It came after the firm forecast quarterly profit and revenue well above expectations.

Micron is a major memory-chip maker and a key supplier to AI titan Nvidia.

The company's shares have more than tripled in value so far this year, and soared close to a fifth following its results.

:: 5%

Almost 5% is how far Apple shares fell after it announced price hikes.

The tech giant said its cheapest laptop will jump from $599 to $699, with iPad prices also rising - although the iPhone won't jump for now.

Apple says it can no longer avoid passing on rising costs to consumers.

The price of memory chips jumped by around 98% in the first quarter, and has kept soaring due to the AI boom.

:: 42% 

42% was how much shares of Wendy's soared as retail traders bought up the beaten-down stock in the latest meme-like rally.

Trading on the heavily shorted stock was paused multiple times due to volatility..

Shares briefly hit a more than seven-month high.

The fast food chain is battling weak sales and pressure from an activist investor, with its stock down more than 78% from record highs five years ago.

:: $648 billion

Almost $648 billion is how much Samsung plans to invest in South Korea over 10 years.

That's according to the Maeil Business Newspaper, which said the firm will announce its intentions on Monday.

The investment includes a potential $194 billion to build chip factories in the country's southwest, as well as plans for AI data centers, batteries and displays.

There was no immediate response to a request for comment from Samsung.

:: $606.5 million

And $606.5 million was H&M's operating profit during its fiscal second quarter.

That was smaller than expected for the Swedish fashion retailer.

H&M blamed weaker consumer demand and inflation made worse by the Iran war.

CEO Daniel Erver said Europe, particularly the western part of it, had gone through a difficult quarter.

content.title: The Week in Numbers: Micron climbs high, Apple raises prices
content.summary:
STORY: From Micron's mega market cap to Apple's price hikes...

...this is the Week in Numbers.

:: The Week in Numbers

:: $1.4 trillion.

About $1.4 trillion was Micron's market cap after it briefly blew past the value of Meta and Tesla for the first time.

It came after the firm forecast quarterly profit and revenue well above expectations.

Micron is a major memory-chip maker and a key supplier to AI titan Nvidia.

The company's shares have more than tripled in value so far this year, and soared close to a fifth following its results.

:: 5%

Almost 5% is how far Apple shares fell after it announced price hikes.

The tech giant said its cheapest laptop will jump from $599 to $699, with iPad prices also rising - although the iPhone won't jump for now.

Apple says it can no longer avoid passing on rising costs to consumers.

The price of memory chips jumped by around 98% in the first quarter, and has kept soaring due to the AI boom.

:: 42% 

42% was how much shares of Wendy's soared as retail traders bought up the beaten-down stock in the latest meme-like rally.

Trading on the heavily shorted stock was paused multiple times due to volatility..

Shares briefly hit a more than seven-month high.

The fast food chain is battling weak sales and pressure from an activist investor, with its stock down more than 78% from record highs five years ago.

:: $648 billion

Almost $648 billion is how much Samsung plans to invest in South Korea over 10 years.

That's according to the Maeil Business Newspaper, which said the firm will announce its intentions on Monday.

The investment includes a potential $194 billion to build chip factories in the country's southwest, as well as plans for AI data centers, batteries and displays.

There was no immediate response to a request for comment from Samsung.

:: $606.5 million

And $606.5 million was H&M's operating profit during its fiscal second quarter.

That was smaller than expected for the Swedish fashion retailer.

H&M blamed weaker consumer demand and inflation made worse by the Iran war.

CEO Daniel Erver said Europe, particularly the western part of it, had gone through a difficult quarter.

headline: Stocks Test ‘Bad News is Good News’ Theory as Tech Rally Wobbles
summary: Financial markets can be funny about ‘bad news’, often using it as a reason to bid up assets as a central narrative is tested by incoming data.  A softer-than-expected jobs report can reduce inflation pressures, lower Treasury yields and lift stocks higher.  The same is true for weak gross domestic product numbers or soft retail sales, which can stoke asset prices even as they signal difficult times ahead.

content.title: Stocks Test ‘Bad News is Good News’ Theory as Tech Rally Wobbles
content.summary: Financial markets can be funny about ‘bad news’, often using it as a reason to bid up assets as a central narrative is tested by incoming data.  A softer-than-expected jobs report can reduce inflation pressures, lower Treasury yields and lift stocks higher.  The same is true for weak gross domestic product numbers or soft retail sales, which can stoke asset prices even as they signal difficult times ahead.

headline: Stock Index Futures Slip as Chip Selloff Resumes, Fed Speak on Tap
summary: September S&P 500 E-Mini futures (ESU26) are down -0.51%, and September Nasdaq 100 E-Mini futures (NQU26) are down -1.18% this morning as renewed selling pressure hit the tech sector, with chipmakers leading the declines.

headline: Apple is raising prices. Will iPhones be next?
summary: Apple raised prices on some of its most popular computer products by as much as $500 on Thursday, citing the increasing costs of memory chips driven by the AI boom. Although the company kept the price of the iPhone steady, analysts predict the new model released this fall will get a price hike. The chip squeeze primarily affects components that store memory, including RAM, a device's temporary working memory, as well as the technology used for long-term storage in phones, tablets and laptops. Su
